column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation ,
column13: Just slighty off the curve on the positive direction which is somewhat what i expected.
column14: Good ADCP mmnt 20 m upstream of the gauge just below the old cableway Stage tracking very well. No SRC to the float. Reset the OTT. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13:
Bit of a negative shift here compared to most years but there does look to be some slumping of the bank on the right side which could be having an effect. Similar shift to the last visit. 
No beaver dam at the moment.
column14: Good wading mmnt downstream of the gauge 30 m at our normal spot.  Stage tracking very well. Well connected and ice free. OTT sensor was slightly out. Reset HG2. Flushed the intakes and recovered well.  Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.656 HG2: 0.648 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Cloudy , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13:
Trace ice left on the edges likely not causing any backwater.
There was some reminants of an old beaver dam on the right side of the control but Rob removed it before the mmnt so the control is pretty much clear moving forward. 
Slight BW shift on this mmnt but i dont think its from any of the trace surface ice on the edges. Could be from bank slumping or some of the beaver reminants we may have missed but similar shift to the July mmnt last season before the beaver dam was built.
column14: Good wading mmnt downstream of the gauge at our normal spot 25 m downstream. No issues.  Still ice in the well. Dropped the float on the top of the ice for when it melts but HG is still not valid. Reading 0.642 currently. HG2 (OTT) is working great and found a very minor SRC though not applied or reset since its within the +/- 0.003 range. Stage dropping decently fast during the visit. Station now operational but HG2 will have to be imported in until the well thaws. Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG2: 0.570 Surge: 0.002  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Cloudy , Light Snow ,
column13:
Ice on edges at gauge, also a partial beaver dam has been built up again on the control.
Should be lots of extra backwater at the moment.
Did not removed the beaver dam this visit since its shutdown anyways. Beavers win this round.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal section downstream of the gauge around 25m.   Stage tracking very well. No SRC applied. Float pinned at 1.815m. HG not valid after this visit. HG2 still valid.  Reset the OTT so it matches the old record perfectly. Station shutdown for the season though HG2 will be copied into the stage working record up until Oct 31. Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.482 HG2: 0.480 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Debris; Clear , No Precipitation ,
column13:
Beavers look to have repaired and started building a dam again creating more backwater. Will Remove after the mmnt.
Should be pretty close to the curve after the beaver dam was removed.
column14: Good wading mmnt downstream of the gauge 25 m at my regular spot. Stage tracking very well. No SRC. Installed an OTT secondary sensor in front of the shelter in the creek. Drop in stage after the mmnt is valid after the beaver dam was removed. Reset both sensors after the stage drop just to get it perfectly on the ECG. Mid-section
HG: 0.476 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Altered; Cloudy , No Precipitation ,
column13:
Small beaver dam looks to have been started on the banks. Might be causing a bit of backwater. 
Removed after the mmnt. Looks like we're back on the curve after removing the dam. Thanks Julia.
column14: Good wading mmnt at the normal spot around 30 m downstream.  Stage tracking very well. No SRC. Drop in stage after mmnt is valid. Removed beaver dam.  Flushed lower intake an recovered well. Mid-section
HG: 0.517 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13:
% mmnt based on this mmnt.
Looks like the high water event did not change the control.
I do see some of the banks slumping in the river on the edges in spots
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal spot 20 m downstream. River way more silty than usual. Assuming from the high water slumping the banks and the river washing it out over time? or maybe a big thunderstorm as there was one early this morning and i found what looked like snow/hail beside the well outside. Stage tracking well after the high water event. Flushed both the upper and lower intake well and seems to have recovered quite well. No silting.  Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.563 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Smokey , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Upstream
column13:
% mmnt. Control clear.
No beaver dam on control.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal spot 30 m below the gauge. 1 "reverse" shot on the left edge. SNR error on the left bank shot due to silt kick up Stage tracking very well. No SRC. Gave both intakes a good flushing since they were frozen on open water. Recovered well. Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.427 No Reset (NR)
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.694 Surge: 0.004  * The uncertainty value is equivalent to the surge value in eHSN.  No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: No ice left in the channel.
column14: Good wading mmnt below the gauge at my normal spot just above the control. Used a DWL for the MGH but it was well after the mmnt since i had to walk back to the truck. % mmnt.  Water over ice in the well. Pretty sure it froze low and the lower intake is incased in ice and likely still froze. Dropped the float and reset the sensor eventhough it was very close.  Pulled a bucket to see if theres any connection and it did not recover so looks like the intakes are still frozen which is rare for this station.  Should open up soon i would think with this heat. Stage had not recovered after the mmnt so stage not valid at the moment. Was able to flush the upper intake and its not frozen but its above the water in the well so the stage is not high enough for it to be active. Tried the lower but the stand pipe is frozen so did nothing.  Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Clear , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13:
No ice on the control but a very trace amount on the surface on the edges thats barely visible.
Similar shift to last visit last week.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal section downstream of the gauge 25 m. Stage tracking very well. No SRC. Flushed the lower intake and recovered well.  Pinned the float @ 1.833 m @ 13:36 Station shutdown for the season.  Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.323 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation ,
column13: Control clear. No beaver dam this visit. Very low water mmnt. Negative shift follows the low end trend.
column14: Good wading mmnt at normal spot 30 m downstream of guage. Stage tracking very well. No SRC.  Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Debris; Clear , No Precipitation ,
column13: Partial beaver dam built on the control again. Removed after the mmnt.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal spot 30 m downstream or there abouts. Stage tracking very well. No SRC. Drop in stage after the mmnt is valid as i removed the beaver dam again.  Pool was still draining on the 2nd water level.  Flushed the lower intake and recovered well.  Mid-section
HG: 0.415 No Reset (NR)
HG: 0.416 No Reset (NR)

column12: Measurement condition : Debris; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13:
Looks like a bit of a start of a beaver dam on the right bank of the control. Will remove after the mmnt. 
Some extra backwater on this mmnt from the start of a beaver dam.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal spot downstream of the gauge around 20 m. Stage tracking vey well. No SRC.  Large drop in stage just after the mmnt is valid as i removed the start of a beaver dam on the right bank of the control  Mid-section
HG: 0.450 No Reset (NR)

column13: % mmnt.
column14: Good wading mmnt at my normal spot just above the control about 20 m downstream. Stage tracking very well.  Flushed the intakes well. Recovered pretty fast.  Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation ,
column13: Control clear. % mmnt.

column12: Measurement condition : Clear; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Control clear but some ice chunks still flowing down the river.
column14: Good wading mmnt at the regular spot downstream of the gauge just above the control. Skim ice in well smashed with bucket but thicker ice below that under the water but was too think to break up by foot. Good connection through the well edges and flushed and recovered so left as is to melt naturally.  Stage changing fairly quickly. Maybe due to ice jamming upstream or just increased melting.  Decent sized ice jam just upstream of the gauge broke free after we were doing our benchmark ties. Still more chucks of flowing ice and changing stages. Mid-section
GC: 0.000 HG: 0.559 Reset (RS)

column13:
Control clear.
No ice.
Similar negative shift to the other low water mmnt done earlier this season.
column14: Good wading mmnt at normal spot 20 m downstream of gauge. Stage tracking very well. Flushed the intakes and recovered well. Pinned the float @ 1.814 m. Station shutdown for the season. Mid-section

I dont see any real veg that would cuase this. More likely the slumping of the banks that happens here.

column14: Good wading mmnt at normal spot 20 m downstream. Slight angle. Edited file after the fact as the edge shot did not take the -1 correction factor in the field.
